1. ā-√gur Ā. -gurate (Impv. 2. sg. -gurasva) to approve, agree or assent to (acc.),  RV. iii, 52, 2 ;  AV. v, 20, 4 ;  TBr. ;
(Pot. -gureta) to pronounce the Āgur (see the next),  AitBr.

results.rendered_html: āgur (von gur = gar, gṛṇāti mit ā) f. zustimmender Ausruf; so heissen Formeln in priesterlichen Responsorien: tadyadevātra maitrāvaruṇo dvirāgurate saiva hoturāgūḥ  AIT. BR. 2,28 . ye3 yajāmaha ityāgūḥ  ĀŚV. ŚR. 1,5 . asya yajñasyāgura udṛcamaśīya 4,3 . = pratijñā  AK. 1,1,4,14 .  H. 278 .  SĀY.  Die Erklärer zu  AK.  stellen aus Missverständniss auch eine Form āgū auf.

results.rendered_html:
āgur f. [ā-gur-kvip]
1 Assent, agreement, promise.
2 N. of a class of plauditory exclamations or formularies used by the priests in sacrificial rites; ye 3 yajāmaha ityāgūḥ  Āśval.
